IRS990.ActivityOrMissionDesc: OLE HEALTH IS THE MEDICAL HOME FOR THE COMMUNITY'S UNDERSERVED PATIENTS.
IRS990.MissionDesc: OLE HEALTH BUILDS STRONGER COMMUNITIES BY ENSURING HIGH QUALITY, COMPREHENSIVE AND PERSONALIZED CARE FOR EVERYONE WHO LIVES AND WORKS IN THE PLACES WE CALL HOME.
IRS990.Desc: OLE HEALTH IS A FEDERALLY QUALIFIED HEALTH CENTER (FQHC), SERVING NAPA, SOLANO AND YOLO COUNTIES. WE PROVIDE PRIMARY MEDICAL CARE, INCLUDING PERINATAL, REPRODUCTIVE HEALTH, PEDIATRIC, ADOLESCENT AND NUTRITION SERVICES, TO OVER 47,816 PATIENTS PER YEAR. ALL OF THESE SERVICES ARE AVAILABLE AT LITTLE TO NO COST FOR PATIENTS THROUGH THE SLIDING FEE SCALE DISCOUNT PROGRAM.

ProgSrvcAccomActy2Grp.Desc: THE DENTAL SERVICES PROGRAM AT OLE HEALTH PROVIDES AFFORDABLE DENTAL CARE TO APPROXIMATELY 13,859 PATIENTS PER YEAR.

ProgSrvcAccomActy3Grp.Desc: THE MENTAL HEALTH PROGRAM AT OLE HEALTH PROVIDES AFFORDABLE MENTAL HEALTH CARE TO 7,652 PATIENTS PER YEAR.

ProgSrvcAccomActyOtherGrp.Desc: OLE HEALTH'S COMPREHENSIVE OPTOMETRY SERVICES INCLUDE DIAGNOSIS AND TREATMENT OF THE EYE AND VISUAL SYSTEM, AND A MODERN OPTICAL DISPENSARY FEATURING THE LATEST DESIGNER EYEWEAR TECHNOLOGY. OPTOMETRY SERVICES INCLUDE: EYE AND VISION EXAMS; RED-EYE EXAMS; FRAME SELECTION; AND AN OPTICAL DEPARTMENT.OTHER PROGRAM SERVICE EXPENSES ALSO INCLUDES GRANTS AND SPONSORSHIPS TO VARIOUS COMMUNITY ORGANIZATIONS.

Form990ScheduleAPartVIGrp.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: SCHEDULE A, PART II, LINE 10, EXPLANATION OF OTHER INCOME:
Form990ScheduleAPartVIGrp.ExplanationTxt: OTHER INCOME - 2019 AMOUNT: $ 739,428. 2020 AMOUNT: $ 60,160. 2021 AMOUNT: $ 177,570. 2022 AMOUNT: $ 688,224. 2023 AMOUNT: $ 288,078. GAIN ON LEASE TERMINATION - 2022 AMOUNT: $ 3,922,027.

SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE ORGANIZATION IS A NONPROFIT CORPORATION UNDER INTERNAL REVENUE CODE SECTION 501(C)(3) AND HAS BEEN GRANTED TAX-EXEMPT STATUS BY THE INTERNAL REVENUE SERVICE AND THE CALIFORNIA REVENUE AND TAXATION CODE. AS OF JUNE 30, 2024 AND 2023, THE ORGANIZATION HAD NO UNRECOGNIZED TAX POSITIONS OR UNCERTAIN TAX POSITIONS REQUIRING ACCRUAL. THEREFORE, NO PROVISION FOR INCOME TAXES HAS BEEN PROVIDED IN THE CONSOLIDATED FINANCIAL STATEMENTS.

CERTAIN INDIVIDUALS APPEARING ON FORM 990, PART VII AND/OR SCHEDULE J, PART II RECEIVED A NON-FIXED PAYMENT IN THE FORM OF A BONUS; IN GENERAL, BONUSES FOR THESE INDIVIDUALS ARE NOT SPECIFIED BY A FIXED FORMULA AND REQUIRE THE USE OF DISCRETION (IN PART) TO DETERMINE WHETHER TO MAKE A BONUS PAYMENT AND/OR THE AMOUNT OF BONUS AWARDED.

TaxExemptBondsIssuesGrp.PurposeDesc: CONSTRUCT & EQUIP FACILITIES; REFUND PRIOR TAXABLE BONDS

SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE ORGANIZATION IS REPORTING THE TOTAL NUMBER OF CONTRIBUTIONS RECEIVED (DEFINED AS EACH SEPARATE GIFT) IN SCHEDULE M, PART I, COLUMN (B).

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION A, LINE 4
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: DURING THE 6/30/2024 FISCAL, THE ORGANIZATION AMENDED ITS BYLAWS. THE SIGNIFICANT CHANGES (AMONGST MANY WORDING CHANGES) AMONG THOSE AMENDMENTS INCLUDE: (I) UPDATING TO INCLUDE THE DBA NAME OF "COMMUNICARE OLE; AND (II) AND REMOVING THE CHIEF MEDICAL OFFICER AS A NAMED OFFICER IN THE BYLAWS.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION B, LINE 11B
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: TO PREPARE THE FORM 990, THE ORGANIZATION UTILIZED A PROFESSIONAL ACCOUNTING FIRM WHO WORKED IN CONJUNCTION WITH THE ORGANIZATION'S FINANCE AND ACCOUNTING TEAM TO GATHER THE REQUIRED INFORMATION. UPON REVIEW OF THE FORM 990 AMONGST THE ORGANIZATION'S MANAGEMENT TEAM, THE FORM 990 WAS THEN SENT TO THE AUDIT & BENEFITS COMMITTEE OF THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS FOR REVIEW AND APPROVAL. THE AUDIT & BENEFITS COMMITTEE ALSO PRESENTS THE RESULTS OF ITS REVIEW PROCESS TO THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS, WHO RECEIVES A COPY OF THE FORM 990 PRIOR TO FILING WITH THE INTERNAL REVENUE SERVICE.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION B, LINE 12C
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: ALL EMPLOYEES AND BOARD MEMBERS MUST COMPLETE AND SIGN A CONFLICT OF INTEREST DISCLOSURE STATEMENT ANNUALLY. IT IS ALSO THE CONTINUING RESPONSIBILITY OF THE BOARD, OFFICERS, AND MANAGEMENT EMPLOYEES TO SCRUTINIZE THEIR TRANSACTIONS, OUTSIDE BUSINESS INTERESTS, AND RELATIONSHIPS FOR POTENTIAL CONFLICTS AND TO IMMEDIATELY MAKE SUCH DISCLOSURES. TRANSACTIONS WITH PARTIES WITH WHOM A CONFLICTING INTEREST EXISTS MAY BE UNDERTAKEN ONLY IF ALL OF THE FOLLOWING ARE OBSERVED: (1) THE CONFLICTING INTEREST IS FULLY DISCLOSED; (2) THE PERSON WITH THE CONFLICT OF INTEREST IS EXCLUDED FROM THE DISCUSSION AND APPROVAL OF SUCH TRANSACTION; (3) A COMPETITIVE BID OR COMPARABLE VALUATION EXISTS; AND (4) THE BOARD HAS DETERMINED THAT THE TRANSACTION IS IN THE BEST INTEREST OF THE ORGANIZATION. DISCLOSURE FROM EMPLOYEES OF THE ORGANIZATION SHOULD BE MADE TO THE CHIEF EXECUTIVE OFFICER (OR IF HE/SHE IS THE ONE WITH THE CONFLICT, THEN TO THE BOARD CHAIR); DISCLOSURES INVOLVING DIRECTORS SHOULD BE MADE TO THE BOARD CHAIR (OR IF HE/SHE IS THE ONE WITH THE CONFLICT, THEN TO THE BOARD VICE CHAIR). CONFLICTED INDIVIDUALS ARE NOT ALLOWED TO PARTICIPATE IN THE DELIBERATION AND VOTE ON ANY MATTER.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION B, LINE 15
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE CEO AND CFO COMPENSATION PACKAGES ARE APPROVED BY THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS AFTER PERFORMANCE REVIEWS AND COMPENSATION STUDIES. COMPENSATION PACKAGES FOR OTHER KEY LEADERSHIP POSITIONS ARE BASED ON A COMPENSATION STUDY DONE BY AN INDEPENDENT FIRM EVERY TWO TO THREE YEARS. IF NECESSARY TO EVALUATE OUTSIDE OF THAT TIME PERIOD, THE HUMAN RESOURCES DEPARTMENT WILL EVALUATE BY SOLICITING EXTERNAL COMPENSATION DATA AND PLACE THE POSITION APPROPRIATELY WITHIN THE ORGANIZATIONAL STRUCTURE.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION C, LINE 19
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE AUDITED FINANCIAL STATEMENTS, CONFLICT OF INTEREST POLICY, AND GOVERNING DOCUMENTS ARE AVAILABLE UPON REQUEST.
INHERENT CONTRIBUTION DUE TO MERGER WITH COMMUNICARE HEALTH CENTERS 46,545,929.

DisregardedEntityName.BusinessNameLine1Txt: NAPA VALLEY VINTNERS COMMUNITY HEALTH CENTER

IdRelatedTaxExemptOrgGrp.PrimaryActivitiesTxt: LEASE OF HEALTHCARE FACILITIES TO OLE HEALTH

OtherOrganizationName.BusinessNameLine1Txt: NAPA VALLEY VINTNERS COMMUNITY HEALTH CENTER
